title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today's signal is concentrated in evaluation, auditing, and governance rather than new model capabilities. The strongest updates push toward more verifiable assessments of agent behavior, stronger privacy and unlearning audits, and clearer standards for monitoring and cyber safety as AI systems move into higher-stakes settings.

items.title: Google proposes a new framework for auditing machine unlearning
items.why_it_matters: Auditable unlearning is central to privacy, compliance, and verifying deletion claims in deployed models.

items.title: OpenAI backs Europe’s AI content transparency and provenance work
items.why_it_matters: Content provenance standards affect disclosure, traceability, and enforcement in trustworthy AI deployment.

items.title: NIST argues AI security should shift to continuous monitoring and updates
items.why_it_matters: It reinforces that static certification is insufficient for managing evolving AI system risks.

items.title: AgentBeats introduces an open framework for standardized agent assessment
items.why_it_matters: Comparable, reproducible agent evaluations are necessary for credible safety claims across systems.

items.title: EvoArena benchmarks how agent memory evolves in dynamic environments
items.why_it_matters: Dynamic-environment testing can reveal drift, stale memory, and brittle behavior missed by static benchmarks.

items.title: One Polluted Page Is Enough examines web-content poisoning in generative recommenders
items.why_it_matters: It highlights a concrete attack surface for retrieval-augmented systems that rely on live web inputs.

items.title: EpiBench offers verifiable evaluation for AI agents doing epigenomics analysis
items.why_it_matters: Verifiable domain benchmarks improve confidence in agent performance for high-consequence scientific workflows.

items.title: Operadic consistency proposes a label-free signal for LLM reasoning failures
items.why_it_matters: Inference-time failure detection could strengthen safeguards when ground truth is unavailable.

items.title: Beyond the IT Checklist argues for a cyber safety standard of care
items.why_it_matters: It reframes cyber governance around harm prevention, not just control documentation and compliance.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
